records.ety: [OE. windewen, winewen, AS. windwian; akin to Goth. winpjan (in comp.), winpi-skauro a fan, L. ventilare to fan, to winnow; cf. L. wannus a fan for winnowing, G. wanne, OHG. wanna. √131. See Wind moving air, and cf. Fan., n., Ventilate.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Winnowed (wn"nd); p. pr. & vb. n. Winnowing.]

defs: To separate, and drive off, the chaff from by means of wind; to fan; as, to winnow grain.
quotes: Ho winnoweth barley to-night in the threshing floor.

defs: To sift, as for the purpose of separating falsehood from truth; to separate, as bad from good.
quotes: Winnow well this thought, and you shall find This light as chaff that flies before the wind.

defs: To beat with wings, or as with wings.
quotes: Now on the polar winds; then with quick fan Winnows the buxom air.
